When the event is a `HIT` the request also accepts a number `duration` which is the time taken to generate the artifact in the cache.

An optional `source` query parameter can be provided to identify the caller. Defaults to `api` if not specified. This is only available for Owner, Member, Developer, Security, and Billing roles for the supplied team. Required: item. Optional: source.

params.name: source

The response is streamed as newline-delimited JSON (JSONL) and can be optionally compressed with gzip if the `Accept-Encoding: gzip` header is provided. tools.name: edge-cache_dangerously-delete-by-src-images
tools.description: Marks a source image as deleted, causing cache entries associated with that source image to be revalidated in the foreground on the next request. Use this method with caution because one source image can be associated with many paths and deleting the cache can cause many concurrent requests to the origin leading to cache stampede problem. This method is for advanced use cases and is not recommended; prefer using `invalidateBySrcImage` instead. Required: projectIdOrName, srcImages. Optional: revalidationDeadlineSeconds.

tools.name: edge-cache_dangerously-delete-by-tags

Use this method with caution because one tag can be associated with many paths and deleting the cache can cause many concurrent requests to the origin leading to cache stampede problem. This method is for advanced use cases and is not recommended; prefer using `invalidateByTag` instead. Required: projectIdOrName, tags. Optional: revalidationDeadlineSeconds, target.

In the case of a successful response you'll receive a status code 200 with an empty body.

tools.name: installations_billing_finalize-installation
tools.description: This endpoint allows the partner to mark an installation as finalized. This means you will not send any more invoices for the installation. Use this after a customer has requested uninstall and you have sent any remaining invoices. This will allow the uninstall process to proceed immediately after all invoices have been paid. Use the `credentials.access_token` we provided in the [Upsert Installation](#upsert-installation) body to authorize this request. Required: integrationConfigurationId.

tools.name: installations_billing_get-invoice

See [Billing Events with Webhooks documentation](https://vercel.com/docs/integrations/create-integration/marketplace-api#working-with-billing-events-through-webhooks) on how to receive invoice events. This endpoint is used to retrieve the invoice details. Required: integrationConfigurationId, invoiceId. Returns the BillingGetInvoiceResponse.

tools.name: installations_billing_submit-data
tools.description: Sends the billing and usage data. The partner should do this at least once a day and ideally once per hour. Use the `credentials.access_token` we provided in the [Upsert Installation](#upsert-installation) body to authorize this request. Required: integrationConfigurationId, billing, eod, period, timestamp, usage.

params.name: eod
params.description: End of Day, the UTC datetime for when the end of the billing/usage day is in UTC time. This tells us which day the usage data is for, and also allows for your 'end of day' to be different from UTC 00:00:00. eod must be within the period dates, and cannot be older than 24h earlier from our server's current time.

params.name: timestamp
params.description: Server time of your integration, used to determine the most recent data for race conditions & updates. Only the latest usage data for a given day, week, and month will be kept.

tools.name: installations_billing_submit-invoice
tools.description: This endpoint allows the partner to submit an invoice to Vercel. The invoice is created in Vercel's billing system and sent to the customer. Depending on the type of billing plan, the invoice can be sent at a time of signup, at the start of the billing period, or at the end of the billing period. Use the `credentials.access_token` we provided in the [Upsert Installation](#upsert-installation) body to authorize this request. There are several limitations to the invoice submission: 1. A resource can only be billed once per the billing period and the billing plan. 2. The billing plan used to bill the resource must have been active for this resource during the billing period. 3. The billing plan used must be a subscription plan. 4. The interim usage data must be sent hourly for all types of subscriptions. See [Send subscription billing and usage data](#send-subscription-billing-and-usage-data) API on how to send interim billing and usage data. 5. If provided, `externalId` must be unique for the installation. . Required: integrationConfigurationId, invoiceDate, items, period. Optional: discounts, externalId, final (plus 2 more). Returns array of string.

params.name: externalId
params.description: Partner-provided invoice identifier. If provided, it must be unique for this installation.

params.name: final
params.description: Set this to `true` if this is the final invoice for the installation. Can only be set when the installation is pending deletion.

params.name: invoiceDate
params.description: Invoice date. Must be within the period's start and end.

tools.name: installations_billing_submit-prepayment-balances
tools.description: Sends the prepayment balances. The partner should do this at least once a day and ideally once per hour. Use the `credentials.access_token` we provided in the [Upsert Installation](#upsert-installation) body to authorize this request. Required: integrationConfigurationId, balances, timestamp.

params.name: timestamp
params.description: Server time of your integration, used to determine the most recent data for race conditions & updates. Only the latest usage data for a given day, week, and month will be kept.

tools.name: installations_billing_update-invoice

tools.name: installations_events_create
tools.description: Partner notifies Vercel of any changes made to an Installation or a Resource. Vercel is expected to use `list-resources` and other read APIs to get the new state. `resource.updated` event should be dispatched when any state of a resource linked to Vercel is modified by the partner. `installation.updated` event should be dispatched when an installation's billing plan is changed via the provider instead of Vercel. Resource update use cases: - The user renames a database in the partner’s application. The partner should dispatch a `resource.updated` event to notify Vercel to update the resource in Vercel’s datastores. - A resource has been suspended due to a lack of use. The partner should dispatch a `resource.updated` event to notify Vercel to update the resource's status in Vercel's datastores. . Required: integrationConfigurationId, event.

tools.name: installations_products_update-resource-secrets
tools.description: This endpoint is deprecated and replaced with the endpoint [Update Resource Secrets](#update-resource-secrets). This endpoint updates the secrets of a resource. If a resource has projects connected, the connected secrets are updated with the new secrets. The old secrets may still be used by existing connected projects because they are not automatically redeployed. Redeployment is a manual action and must be completed by the user. All new project connections will use the new secrets. Use cases for this endpoint: - Resetting the credentials of a database in the partner. If the user requests the credentials to be updated in the partner’s application, the partner post the new set of secrets to Vercel, the user should redeploy their application and the expire the old credentials. . Required: integrationConfigurationId, integrationProductIdOrSlug, resourceId, secrets. Optional: partial.

params.name: partial
params.description: If true, will only update the provided secrets

tools.name: integrations_exchange-sso-token
tools.description: During the autorization process, Vercel sends the user to the provider [redirectLoginUrl](https://vercel.com/docs/integrations/create-integration/submit-integration#redirect-login-url), that includes the OAuth authorization `code` parameter. The provider then calls the SSO Token Exchange endpoint with the sent code and receives the OIDC token. They log the user in based on this token and redirects the user back to the Vercel account using deep-link parameters included the redirectLoginUrl. Providers should not persist the returned `id_token` in a database since the token will expire. See [**Authentication with SSO**](https://vercel.com/docs/integrations/create-integration/marketplace-api#authentication-with-sso) for more details. (public)

tools.name: projects_protection-bypass_update-project

tools.name: projects_rollback_request

tools.name: projects_rolling-release_approve-stage
tools.description: Advance a rollout to the next stage. This is only needed when rolling releases is configured to require manual approval. Required: idOrName, canaryDeploymentId, nextStageIndex. Returns the new RollingReleaseApproveStageResponse.

params.name: canaryDeploymentId

params.description: The index of the stage to transition to

tools.name: projects_rolling-release_complete

tools.name: projects_rolling-release_delete-config
tools.description: Disable Rolling Releases for a project means that future deployments will not undergo a rolling release. Changing the config never alters a rollout that's already in-flight—it only affects the next production deployment. If you want to also stop the current rollout, call this endpoint to disable the feature, and then call either the /complete or /abort endpoint. Required: idOrName. Returns the RollingReleaseDeleteConfigResponse. Destructive.

tools.name: projects_rolling-release_get
tools.description: Return the Rolling Release for a project, regardless of whether the rollout is active, aborted, or completed. If the feature is enabled but no deployment has occurred yet, null will be returned. Required: idOrName. Optional: state. Returns the RollingReleaseGetResponse.

tools.name: projects_rolling-release_get-billing-status
tools.description: Get the Rolling Releases billing status for a project. The team level billing status is used to determine if the project can be configured for rolling releases. Required: idOrName.

tools.name: projects_rolling-release_get-config
tools.description: Get the Rolling Releases configuration for a project. The project-level config is simply a template that will be used for any future rolling release, and not the configuration for any active rolling release. Required: idOrName. Returns the RollingReleaseGetConfigResponse.

tools.name: projects_rolling-release_update-config
tools.description: Update (or disable) Rolling Releases for a project. When disabling with the resolve-on-disable feature flag enabled, any active rolling release document is resolved using the disableRolloutAction parameter: 'abort' to roll back (default), or 'complete' to promote the canary to production. When enabling or updating config, changes only affect the next production deployment and do not alter a rollout that's already in-flight. Note: Enabling Rolling Releases automatically enables skew protection on the project with the default value if it wasn't configured already. Required: idOrName. Partial update.

params.name: route-src-syntax

tools.name: projects_routes_edit

params.name: route-src-syntax
params.description: Pattern syntax type. If not provided, inferred from pattern.

tools.name: projects_routes_get

tools.name: projects_routes_get-versions
tools.description: Get the version history for a project's routing rules. Returns the staging version (if one exists) followed by production versions, most recent first. The staging version has `isStaging: true` and the current production version has `isLive: true`. Required: projectId.

tools.name: projects_routes_stage

tools.name: projects_routes_update-versions
tools.description: Promote staged routing rules to production, restore a previous production version, or discard staged changes. - `promote`: Publishes the staging version to production. - `restore`: Rolls back to a previous production version. - `discard`: Removes the staging version without publishing. Required: projectId, action, id. Returns the new RoutesUpdateVersionsResponse.

tools.name: projects_shared-connect-links_update-static-ips

params.name: idOrName

Users can also accept the project transfer request using the claim URL: `https://vercel.com/claim-deployment?code= &returnUrl=<returnUrl>`. The `code` parameter specifies the project transfer request code generated using this endpoint. tools.name: sandboxes_create
tools.description: Creates a named sandbox environment. Named sandboxes have a unique name within a project and support automatic snapshotting on shutdown. Optional: env (default: map[]), keepLastSnapshots, mounts (plus 11 more). Returns array of SandboxPublicRoute.

params.name: env
params.description: Default environment variables for the sandbox. These are inherited by all commands unless overridden.

params.name: keep-last-snapshots-count
params.description: Number of most recent snapshots to keep.

params.name: keep-last-snapshots-delete-evicted
params.description: Whether to immediately delete evicted snapshots. Defaults to true.

params.name: keep-last-snapshots-expiration
params.description: Expiration time in milliseconds for kept snapshots. Falls back to snapshotExpiration.

params.name: mounts
params.description: List of drives to mount to the sandbox at the provided path.

params.name: name
params.description: Name for the sandbox. Must be unique per project and URL-safe (alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores).

params.name: persistent
params.description: Whether the sandbox persists its state across restarts via automatic snapshots. Defaults to true.

params.name: ports
params.description: List of ports to expose from the sandbox. Each port will be accessible via a unique URL. Maximum of 15 ports can be exposed.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The target project slug or ID in which the sandbox will be assigned to.

params.name: resources-memory
params.description: The amount of memory in megabytes to allocate to the sandbox. Must equal vcpus * 2048.

params.name: resources-vcpus
params.description: The number of virtual CPUs to allocate to the sandbox. Must be 1, or an even number.

params.name: runtime
params.description: The runtime environment for the sandbox. Determines the pre-installed language runtimes and tools available.

params.name: snapshotExpiration
params.description: Default snapshot expiration time in milliseconds. Set to 0 to disable expiration. When set, this value is used as the default expiration for all snapshots created for this sandbox.

params.name: source
params.description: The source from which to initialize the sandbox filesystem. Can be a Git repository, a tarball URL, or an existing snapshot.

params.name: tags
params.description: Key-value tags to associate with the sandbox. Maximum 5 tags.

params.name: timeout
params.description: Maximum duration in milliseconds that the sandbox can run before being automatically stopped.

tools.name: sandboxes_create-session-directory
tools.description: Creates a new directory in a session's filesystem. By default, parent directories are created recursively if they don't exist (similar to `mkdir -p`). Required: sessionId, path. Optional: cwd, recursive (default: true). Returns the new SandboxesCreateSessionDirectoryResponse.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the session to create the directory in.

params.name: cwd
params.description: The base directory for resolving relative paths. If not specified, paths are resolved from the sandbox home directory.

params.name: path
params.description: The path of the directory to create. Can be absolute or relative to the working directory.

params.name: recursive
params.description: If true, creates parent directories as needed (like `mkdir -p`). If false, fails if parent directories do not exist.

tools.name: sandboxes_create-session-snapshot
tools.description: Creates a point-in-time snapshot of a running session's filesystem. Snapshots can be used to quickly restore a session to a previous state or to create new sessions with pre-configured environments. The session must be running and able to accept commands for a snapshot to be created. The session will be terminated after the snapshot is created. Required: sessionId. Optional: expiration. Returns the new SandboxesCreateSessionSnapshotResponse.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the session to snapshot.

params.name: expiration
params.description: The number of milliseconds after which the snapshot will expire and be deleted. Use 0 for no expiration.

tools.name: sandboxes_delete-drive
tools.description: Deletes a drive by project and name. Attached drives cannot be deleted. Stop or replace the session currently using the drive before retrying deletion. Drives are in private beta. Register your interest to get access: https://vercel.com/changelog/drives-for-vercel-sandbox-in-private-beta. Required: name. Optional: projectId. Returns the SandboxesDeleteDriveResponse. Destructive.

params.name: name
params.description: Name for the drive. Must be unique per project and URL-safe (alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores).

params.name: projectId
params.description: The project ID or name associated with the drive. Required unless using a Vercel OIDC token scoped to a project.

tools.name: sandboxes_delete-sandbox
tools.description: Deletes a sandbox by name. If sandboxes are currently running, they will be stopped first. This operation deletes all sandbox entities with the given name and the named sandbox metadata. Required: name. Optional: projectId. Returns the SandboxesDeleteSandboxResponse. Destructive.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The project ID that owns the named sandbox. When provided, takes precedence over OIDC project context.

tools.name: sandboxes_delete-session-snapshot
tools.description: Permanently deletes a snapshot and frees its associated storage. This action cannot be undone. After deletion, the snapshot can no longer be used to create new sessions. Required: snapshotId. Returns the SandboxesDeleteSessionSnapshotResponse. Destructive.

params.name: snapshotId
params.description: The unique identifier of the snapshot to delete.

tools.name: sandboxes_extend-session-timeout
tools.description: Extends the maximum execution time of a running session. The session must be active and able to accept commands. The total timeout cannot exceed the maximum allowed limit for your account. Required: sessionId, duration. Returns the new SandboxesExtendSessionTimeoutResponse.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the session to extend the timeout for.

params.name: duration
params.description: The amount of time in milliseconds to add to the current timeout. Must be at least 1000ms (1 second).

tools.name: sandboxes_get-named-sandbox
tools.description: Retrieves a named sandbox by name, including its current sandbox and routes. Returns array of SandboxPublicRoute.

params.name: name
params.description: Name for the sandbox. Must be unique per project and URL-safe (alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores).

params.name: projectId
params.description: The project ID or name (required when not using OIDC token).

params.name: resume
params.description: Whether to automatically resume a stopped named sandbox by creating a new instance from its snapshot. Defaults to false.

tools.name: sandboxes_get-or-create-drive
tools.description: Gets an existing drive by project and name, or creates it when it does not exist. Drives are in private beta. Register your interest to get access: https://vercel.com/changelog/drives-for-vercel-sandbox-in-private-beta. Required: name. Optional: maxSizeBytes, projectId. Returns the new SandboxesGetOrCreateDriveResponse.

params.name: name
params.description: Name for the drive. Must be unique per project and URL-safe (alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores).

params.name: maxSizeBytes
params.description: Maximum drive size in bytes. Defaults to 100 GiB when omitted.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The project ID or name to associate the drive with. Required unless using a Vercel OIDC token scoped to a project.

tools.name: sandboxes_get-session
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific session, including its current status, resource configuration, and exposed routes. Required: sessionId. Returns array of SandboxPublicRoute.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the session to retrieve.

tools.name: sandboxes_get-session-command
tools.description: Retrieves the current status and details of a command executed in a session. Use the `wait` parameter to block until the command finishes execution. Required: sessionId, cmdId. Optional: wait (default: false). Returns the SandboxesGetSessionCommandResponse.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the session containing the command.

params.name: cmdId
params.description: The unique identifier of the command to retrieve.

params.name: wait
params.description: If set to 'true', the request will block until the command finishes execution. Useful for synchronously waiting for command completion.

tools.name: sandboxes_get-session-command-logs
tools.description: Streams the output of a command in real-time using newline-delimited JSON (ND-JSON). Each entry includes the output data and stream type. Stream types include `stdout`, `stderr`, and `error` (for stream failures). Required: sessionId, cmdId.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the session containing the command.

params.name: cmdId
params.description: The unique identifier of the command to stream logs for.

tools.name: sandboxes_get-session-snapshot
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific snapshot, including its creation time, size, expiration date, and the source session it was created from. Required: snapshotId. Returns the SandboxesGetSessionSnapshotResponse.

params.name: snapshotId
params.description: The unique identifier of the snapshot to retrieve.

tools.name: sandboxes_kill-session-command
tools.description: Sends a signal to terminate a running command in a session. The signal can be used to gracefully stop (SIGTERM) or forcefully kill (SIGKILL) the process. params.name: cmdId
params.description: The unique identifier of the command to terminate.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the session containing the command.

params.name: signal

tools.name: sandboxes_run-session-command
tools.description: Executes a shell command inside a running session. The command runs asynchronously and returns immediately with a command ID that can be used to track its progress and retrieve its output. Optionally, use the `wait` parameter to stream the command status until completion. Required: sessionId, cmdId, command. Optional: args, cwd, env (default: map[]) (plus 4 more).

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the session in which to execute the command.

params.name: cmdId
params.description: The unique identifier of the command to stream logs for.

params.name: args
params.description: Arguments to pass to the command. Each argument should be a separate array element.

params.name: command
params.description: The executable or shell command to run. This is the program name without arguments.

params.name: cwd
params.description: The working directory in which to execute the command. Defaults to the sandbox home directory if not specified.

params.name: env
params.description: Additional environment variables to set for this command. These are merged with the sandbox environment.

params.name: logs
params.description: If true, stream the logs of the command execution in real-time via ND-JSON. This is only applicable if `wait` is also true.

params.name: sudo
params.description: Execute the command with root (superuser) privileges.

params.name: timeout
params.description: Maximum duration in milliseconds the command may run before it is killed with SIGKILL. Enforced at exec time, independently of `wait`.

params.name: wait
params.description: If true, returns an ND-JSON stream that emits the command status when started and again when finished. Useful for synchronously waiting for command completion.

tools.name: sandboxes_stop-session
tools.description: Stops a running session and releases its allocated resources. All running processes within the session will be terminated. This action cannot be undone. A stopped session cannot be restarted. Required: sessionId.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the session to stop.

tools.name: sandboxes_update-sandbox
tools.description: Updates the configuration of a sandbox. Only the provided fields will be modified; omitted fields remain unchanged. Required: name. Optional: projectId, resume (default: false), currentSnapshotId (plus 10 more). Partial update.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The project ID that owns the named sandbox. When provided, takes precedence over OIDC project context.

params.name: resume
params.description: Whether to automatically resume a stopped named sandbox by creating a new instance from its snapshot. Defaults to false.

params.name: currentSnapshotId
params.description: The snapshot ID to set as the current snapshot. Must be active and belong to the same project.

params.name: env
params.description: Default environment variables for the sandbox. Set to empty object to clear.

params.name: keepLastSnapshots
params.description: Protect the N most recent snapshots with different expiration/deletion behavior. Set to null to clear.

params.name: persistent
params.description: Whether the sandbox persists its state across restarts via automatic snapshots.

params.name: ports
params.description: List of ports to expose from the sandbox. Each port will be accessible via a unique URL. Maximum of 15 ports can be exposed.

params.name: resources-memory
params.description: The amount of memory in megabytes to allocate to the sandbox. Must equal vcpus * 2048.

params.name: resources-vcpus
params.description: The number of virtual CPUs to allocate to the sandbox. Must be 1, or an even number.

params.name: runtime
params.description: The runtime environment for the sandbox. Determines the pre-installed language runtimes and tools available.

params.name: snapshotExpiration
params.description: Default snapshot expiration time in milliseconds. Set to 0 to disable expiration. When set, this value is used as the default expiration for all snapshots created for this sandbox.

params.name: tags
params.description: Key-value tags to associate with the sandbox. Replaces existing tags. Set to empty object to clear. Maximum 5 tags.

params.name: timeout
params.description: Maximum duration in milliseconds that the sandbox can run before being automatically stopped.

tools.description: Uploads and extracts files to a session's filesystem. Files must be uploaded as a gzipped tarball (`.tar.gz`) with the `Content-Type` header set to `application/gzip`. The tarball contents are extracted to the session's working directory, or to a custom directory specified via the `x-cwd` header. Required: sessionId. Returns the new SandboxesWriteSessionFilesResponse.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the session to write files to.
